questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a difficulty in setting SHE?
options: To bring the reaction in reverse direction
questions.solution: In standard hydrogen electrode (SHE), a platinum plate coated with platinum black is used as electrode. The platinum black is capable of adsorbing large quantities of $\mathrm{H}_2$ gas. This allows the change from gaseous to ionic form and the reverse process to occur.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If a solution contain different type of ions, then fraction of current carried by each ion is different. What must be the ratio of concentration of HCl and NaCl in a solution if the fraction of current carried by $\mathrm{H}^{+}$is 0.5 . (Given $\lambda_{\mathrm{M}}$ of $\mathrm{Na}^{+}, \mathrm{H}^{+}$and $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$are $50 \mathrm{Scm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}, 350 \mathrm{Scm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and $76 \mathrm{Scm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ respectively)
questions.solution:
Let concentration of HCl and NaCl are $\mathrm{C}_1$ and $\mathrm{C}_2$
So $\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]=\mathrm{C}_1,\left[\mathrm{Na}^{+}\right]=\mathrm{C}_2$
$\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]=\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2$
Fraction of current carried by $\mathrm{H}^{+}=0.5$
So, $0.5=\frac{\mathrm{C}_1 \lambda\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]}{\mathrm{C}_1 \lambda\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]+\mathrm{C}_2 \lambda\left[\mathrm{Na}^{+}\right]+\left(\mathrm{C}_1+\mathrm{C}_2\right) \lambda\left[\mathrm{Cl}^{-}\right]}$
$\frac{C_1}{C_2}=\frac{50+76}{350-76}=0.46$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the electrochemical cell
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Zn}\left|\mathrm{Zn}_{0.01 \mathrm{M}, 1 \mathrm{~L}}^{\mathrm{LL}_{0.11} \mathrm{Ag}^{+1 \mathrm{~L}}}\right| \mathrm{Ag} \\
& \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Ag}^{+} / \mathrm{Ag}}^{\circ}=0.8 \mathrm{~V} \\
& \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}^{+2} / \mathrm{Zn}}^{\circ}=-0.77 \mathrm{~V}
\end{aligned}$

If $x \mathrm{~L}$ of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is added in anodic chamber and $y \mathrm{~L}$ of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ is added in cathodic chamber such that emf of cell is more than $E_{c e l l}^{\circ}$. The possible value of $x$ and $y$ are
questions.solution:
Hint : $\mathrm{E}=\mathrm{E}^{\circ}+\frac{0.059}{2} \log \frac{\left[\mathrm{Ag}^{+}\right]^2}{[\mathrm{Zn}]^2}$
Solution :
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{E}=\mathrm{E}^{\circ}+\frac{0.059}{2} \log \frac{\left(\frac{0.1}{\mathrm{~V}_1}\right)^2}{\left(\frac{0.01}{\mathrm{~V}_2}\right)} \\
& \mathrm{E}=\mathrm{E}^{\circ}+\frac{0.059}{2} \log \frac{\mathrm{~V}_2}{\mathrm{~V}_1^2} \\
& \mathrm{E}\gt\mathrm{E}^{\circ}, \mathrm{V}_2\gt\mathrm{V}_1^2 \\
& \Rightarrow(1+\mathrm{x})\gt(1+\mathrm{y})^2
\end{aligned}$
$V_1 \rightarrow$ volume of solution of cathode chamber.
$\mathrm{V}_2 \rightarrow$ volume of solution of Anode chamber.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 0.007 M aqueous solution of aniline hydrochloride had molar conductivity equal to $119.4 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$, which became $103 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$, when a few drops of aniline were added to the solution. Molar conductivity of Hydrochloric acid in the same ionic concentration was found to be $413 \mathrm{~S} \mathrm{~cm}^2 \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. The Hydrolysis constant of aniline hydrochloride is (approx)
questions.solution:
$\alpha=\frac{\Lambda_1-\wedge_2}{\Lambda_3-\Lambda_2}$
$\wedge_1 \Rightarrow$ Molar conductivity of Salt
${ }_2 \Rightarrow$ Molar conductivity of Salt + Base
$\wedge_3 \Rightarrow$ Molar conductivity of Acid
$\alpha=\frac{(119.4)-(103)}{(413)-(103)}=0.052$
$\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{h}}=\frac{\mathrm{ch}^2}{1-\alpha} \approx 2 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{M}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Calculate the potential of an indicator electrode versus the standard hydrogen electrode, which originally contained $0.1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$and $0.1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{H}^{+}$and which was treated with $\mathrm{Fe}^{+2}$ necessary to reduce $90 \%$ of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ to $\mathrm{Mn}^{+2} \cdot \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} / \mathrm{Mn}^{+2}}^0=1.51 \mathrm{~V}$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}+\mathrm{Fe}^{+2}+8 \mathrm{H}^{\oplus} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{+2}+\mathrm{Fe}^{+3}$ 0.1 M $\begin{array}{ll}0.01 \mathrm{M} & 0.09 \mathrm{M}\end{array}$
So permanganate electrode behave as cathode. $E_{c e l l}=1.51-0=1.51$ Using Nernst's equation :- $(\mathrm{n}=5)$ $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-} / \mathrm{Mn}^{\prime 2}}=1.51+\frac{0.059}{5} \log \frac{\left[\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}\right]\left[\mathrm{H}^{+}\right]^8}{\left[\mathrm{Mn}^{+2}\right]}$ $=1.51+0.11=1.4 \mathrm{~V}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Find the standard cell potential involving the cell reaction:
$\mathrm{In}^{2+}+\mathrm{Cu}^{+2} \longrightarrow \mathrm{In}^{+3}+\mathrm{Cu}^{+} \text {, at } 298 \mathrm{~K}$
Given: $E_{\mathrm{Cu}^{-2} / \mathrm{Cu}^{+}}^0=\mathrm{X}_1 \mathrm{~V}, E_{\mathrm{ln}^{-3} / \mathrm{n}^{+}}^0=\mathrm{X}_2 \mathrm{~V}$
$\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{mn}^{-2} \ln n^{+}}^{\circ}=X_3 V$
